concept: Obligation / Requirement Borne by an Agent Under a Stated Source
description: A requirement borne under a stated source, whether inherited, imposed, or tied to a role someone did not choose. Naming the source is what tells an obligation apart from a wish or a private commitment freely made.
sound_symbolism: Necessity 'na' enters the complete commitment-bearing form 'seru', giving the required action continuity without pretending the bearer chose it freely. The open ending carries duty while leaving conflict, refusal, incapacity, and disputed source speakable.
grammatical_notes: A countable or mass noun commonly possessed by its bearer or role: 'ha sithora ha naseru phelu.' (This role has this obligation.) State source, required action, bearer, conditions, duration, conflict, capacity, and consequence. Distinct from 'na' (necessity), 'seru' (commit), 'thonai' (willingly responsible), permission, and 'lothoni' (accountable).

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Obligations are treated as conditioned relations with sources, conflicts, capacities, and consequences rather than as permanent essences of a person.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Naming imposed or role-based obligation separately from commitment and consent prevents institutional demands from borrowing the language of voluntary care.

tags.community: a requirement attributed to an agent under a stated source
